name: interp_aux_equal_from_entries
code:
proof fn phi_1_interp_aux_equal_from_entries(d1: Directory, d2: Directory)
    requires
        d1.inv(),
        d2.inv(),
        d1.arch == d2.arch,
        d1.layer == d2.layer,
        d1.base_vaddr == d2.base_vaddr,
        forall|j: nat| j < d1.entries.len()
            ==> (#[trigger] d1.entries[j as int]).interp(d1.entry_base(j)) == d2.entries[j as int].interp(d1.entry_base(j)),
    ensures
        d1.interp_aux(0) === d2.interp_aux(0),
{
    d1.lemma_entries_interp_equal_implies_interp_aux_equal(d2, 0);
}
reason: Two directories with same entry interpretations have the same interp — direct consequence of the verified lemma

name: interp_aux_tail_empty
code:
proof fn phi_2_interp_aux_tail_empty(d: Directory)
    requires
        d.inv(),
    ensures
        d.interp_aux(d.entries.len()) === Map::<nat, PTE>::empty(),
{
}
reason: `interp_aux` past the end of entries returns empty map — tests the base case of the recursive definition

name: page_entry_singleton_map
reason: A page entry interprets as a singleton map keyed by its base — tests the open spec definition

name: invalid_entry_empty_map
reason: An invalid entry interprets as the empty map — tests the open spec definition

name: directory_entry_ignores_base
reason: A directory entry's interpretation ignores the `base` parameter — `NodeEntry::Directory(d)` calls `d.interp_aux(0)` discarding `base`, which may cause the parent's entry_base to be silently dropped
